canvas.reset:
Reset the application:
• window size and position
• dark mode → light
• thumbnail size → normal
• sidebar → visible / 220 px
• language → automatic detection
• font size → default

animated_gif_in_comic.title: Animated GIF detected
animated_gif_in_comic.message:
Warning: This comic contains one or more animated GIFs.

Comic readers (CDisplayEx, YACReader, etc.) will only display the first frame of each GIF as a static page.

To view the animated GIF properly, you must save it separately outside the comic using the "Export selected pages" or "Edit animated GIF" button.

Do you want to continue saving the comic anyway?

no_selection_adjust.title: No Selection

invalid_selection_adjust.title: Invalid Selection
invalid_selection_adjust.message: Adjustments can only be applied to images.

renumber_disabled_in_subdirectory.title: Feature not available

Renumbering is not available until the folders have been flattened.

labels.license_text:
Copyright © 2025-2026 Bruno Aublet

Summary of the GNU General Public License v3

GRANTED FREEDOMS
You may use, copy, modify and redistribute
MosaicView, even for commercial purposes.

OBLIGATIONS
Any redistribution (original or modified) must :
– retain the GPL v3 license
– include the source code, or indicate where to obtain it
– not impose additional restrictions

WARRANTIES
This software is distributed WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY.
Its author cannot be held liable for damages resulting from its use.

You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along with this program. If not, see <https://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.
labels.license_unrar_text:
CBZ files are based on the ZIP format, a free and open format. CBR files, on the other hand, are based on the RAR format, a proprietary format.

MosaicView can open CBR files thanks to UnRAR, a utility developed by RARlab and freely redistributable.

Official website: <https://www.rarlab.com/rar_add.htm>
labels.empty_canvas_line1: Drop here one or more CBZ/CBR/CB7/PDF files or your images
labels.empty_canvas_line2: Once a file is open, you can continue to drop others
labels.empty_canvas_line3: Drop one or more folders to start a batch process
labels.license_7zip_text:
CB7 files are based on the 7z format, developed by Igor Pavlov and distributed under the GNU LGPL license.

MosaicView can open CB7 files thanks to 7-Zip, a free and open-source utility.

Official website : <https://www.7-zip.org/>

• Horizontally (left/right) for horizontal stitching
join.size_adjustment_warning:
⚠️ The selected images have different dimensions.
Please choose how to adjust their sizes for joining.
The aspect ratio of each image will always be preserved.

pdf.dpi_original_tooltip:
Intelligent analysis of each PDF page:
• Image-only pages: preserves original DPI
• Text-only pages: 300 DPI
• Mixed pages (text + images): image DPI if >= 300, otherwise 300 DPI
Maximum: 2400 DPI to limit file size

reduce_size.title: Resizing {count} {word}
reduce_size.different_dimensions_warning: Selected pages have different dimensions. Custom dimensions will force all pages to the same size. Make sure you understand the implications before continuing.
reduce_size.page_width_info: The most common page widths are 1024, 1280, 1600, 1920, and 2000 pixels, but other values are also suitable.
reduce_size.multi_page_width: Automatically detect and resize multiple pages (width/height)
reduce_size.multi_page_width_tooltip: Enables automatic detection of multiple pages: double page, triple page, etc. The multiplier coefficient is automatically calculated based on each page's ratio, whether in width (side-by-side pages) or height (stacked pages)

duplicate_filenames.title: ⚠️ Duplicate filenames
duplicate_filenames.message:
Identical filenames have been detected. Although saving is technically
possible, this may cause reading issues in some comic book reader
applications (incomplete display, missing pages, etc.).

It is recommended to renumber the pages before saving.

adjustments.title: Adjustments for {count} {word}
adjustments.compression_info: Compression applies only to JPG and WebP images
adjustments.preview_warning: Preview may differ from final result
adjustments.transparency_info: Transparency applies only to PNG, WebP and ICO images
adjustments.transparency_click_instruction: Click on the color to make it transparent
adjustments.transparency_type_flood_tooltip: Flood fill : makes the uniform color area around the clicked pixel transparent
adjustments.transparency_type_global_tooltip: Global transparency : affects all pixels in the image whose color is close to the clicked one
adjustments.transparency_skipped_warning: {count} image(s) skipped — format not compatible with transparency. To include them, convert them to PNG first.
adjustments.auto_levels_tooltip:
Analyses each page and automatically adjusts the black and white levels to maximise contrast.
To choose page by page, use the viewer.

levels_viewer.title: Levels adjustment with eyedroppers
levels_viewer.black_pipette_tooltip: Click on a pixel that should be completely black. Caution: it's easy to overdo it!
levels_viewer.white_pipette_tooltip: Click on a pixel that should be completely white. Caution: it's easy to overdo it!

first_page_multi.option_exclude_desc: The page keeps its current name and stays in first position. You can rename it manually later.

ico_creator.save_blocked_message:
This archive contains one or more .ico files that are not comic book images. Saving is disabled as long as these files are present in the mosaic.
To re-enable saving, delete them from the mosaic (Del key) or cut them (Ctrl+X) to paste them elsewhere.

video_save_blocked.title: Cannot save
video_save_blocked.message:
This archive contains one or more video files. Saving is disabled as long as these files are present in the mosaic.
To re-enable saving, delete them from the mosaic (Del key) or cut them (Ctrl+X) to paste them elsewhere.

tooltip.cbr_info:
CBR files use the RAR format, which is proprietary and patented;
they cannot be modified, changes are saved to a new CBZ file.
tooltip.theme_button: Switch between light and dark theme
tooltip.batch_pdf_to_cbz:
Converts all PDF files in a directory and its
subdirectories to CBZ. Images are extracted and
archived as-is, without any recompression.
tooltip.batch_pdf_permanent_delete:
WARNING: if this option is checked,

of recovery. Make sure you have
a backup if necessary.
tooltip.batch_cbr_to_cbz:
Converts all CBR files in a directory
and its subdirectories to CBZ.
tooltip.batch_cbr_permanent_delete:
WARNING: if this option is checked,

Auto-detect multiple pages mode

tooltip.renumber_btn_2:
Simple enumeration mode

tooltip.batch_img_to_cbz:
Converts all image files found in a folder
and its subfolders to CBZ,
creating one CBZ file per image.
tooltip.batch_img_permanent_delete:
WARNING : if this option is checked,
the original image files will be permanently
deleted after conversion, with no possibility
of recovery. Make sure you have a backup
if necessary.

help.title: MosaicView - User Manual
help.intro: MosaicView allows you to manipulate and edit comics (digital comics) without having to extract them from their archives. The application supports CBZ (ZIP), CBR (RAR) and PDF formats. The application is compatible only with 64-bit systems.
help.open_files_content:
• Click the 'Open CBZ/CBR/CB7/PDF' button
• Or drag and drop a file directly into the window
• Supported formats: CBZ, CBR, CB7, PDF, images (JPG, PNG, WEBP, BMP, TIFF, GIF, ICO). EPUB files can also be opened, but only their images will be extracted (text is ignored).
• Encrypted PDF (password required to open): the file cannot be imported, an error message is displayed.
• PDF protected against modifications (owner password): pages are imported normally. MosaicView offers to save an unlocked copy of the file in the same folder.
help.mosaic_content:
The mosaic displays all pages of your comic book as thumbnails. You can:
• Rearrange pages by drag-and-drop (page renumbering will be automatic, and will follow the active renumbering mode)
• Select multiple pages (Ctrl+click or Shift+click)
• Adjust thumbnail size with the slider
• Manually rename files by clicking on their name
• Drag-and-drop comic book files (CBZ/CBR/PDF) from Windows Explorer directly into another open comic book to merge their content
help.manipulation_content:
• Drag and drop pages to reorganize them
• Drag an entire comic into another to merge them
• Right-click on a page to access options (rotation, deletion, etc.)
• Double-click on a page to open the viewer
• Export individual pages by dragging them to a Windows folder, or via Ctrl+C / Ctrl+V
help.renumber_content:
MosaicView offers two page renumbering modes, accessible via the renumbering button, the context menu (right-click), or automatically during drag and drop:

• Auto-detection of multiple pages mode (default mode): the application analyzes the width of each page to detect double, triple pages, etc. Multiple pages are renumbered accordingly (e.g.: a double page will be named 003-004, a triple 005-006-007). Beyond 4 pages, only the first and last number are shown (e.g.: 010---025).

• Simple enumeration mode: each page receives a unique sequential number (001, 002, 003...), regardless of page width.

To switch between the two modes, right-click on the renumbering button. A tooltip indicates the active mode. The selected mode also applies to automatic renumbering during drag and drop.
help.flatten_content: If your archive contains subdirectories, use 'Flatten directories' to bring all images to the same level.
help.viewer_content:
Double-click on a page to open the viewer:
• ← → keys: navigate between pages
• D key: switch between 3 reading modes
  - Single page: one page at a time
  - Double pages: page 1 alone, then 2-3, 4-5, etc.
  - Continuous double pages: page 1 alone, then 2-3, 3-4, 4-5, etc.
• Mouse wheel: quick navigation
• Ctrl+Wheel or Ctrl+Plus/Minus: zoom
• Ctrl+0: reset zoom
• F11 key: fullscreen
• Double-click: toggle fullscreen
• Esc key: cancel crop frame / exit viewer
help.join_pages_content: Select multiple consecutive pages, right-click → 'Join selected pages' to join pages horizontally or vertically. The output image format matches the source pages' format if they are all identical, otherwise PNG by default.
help.split_pages_content:
To automatically split double pages:
• Select the pages to split
• Right-click → 'Split selected pages'
• Choose the direction (vertical or horizontal)
help.crop_content:
In the viewer, draw a rectangle on the image to define a crop area:
• Draw the rectangle with the mouse
• Resize it by dragging the edges or corners
• Click 'Validate crop' to apply
• The crop is applied to the current image
• Esc key: cancel crop frame
help.shortcuts_content:
• Ctrl+Z: Undo last action
• Ctrl+Y: Redo undone action
• Ctrl+A: Select all pages
• Ctrl+I: Invert selection
• Ctrl+C: Copy selected pages
• Ctrl+X: Cut selected pages
• Ctrl+V: Paste pages
• Del: Delete selected pages
• F5: Update the mosaic display
• F11: Toggle fullscreen (exit with F11 or ESC)
help.dark_mode_content: Click the sun/moon button to switch between light and dark themes.
help.sort_content:
Use the 'Sort' button to automatically reorganize pages by:
• Name, Type, Size, Width, Height, Resolution or DPI
help.save_content:
• 'Save': saves changes to the original file
• 'Save as': creates a new archive
• 'Create CBZ': available in images-only mode (without archive)
help.other_content:
• Rotation: rotate one or more pages
• Conversion: convert images to different formats (PNG, JPEG, WEBP, BMP, TIFF, GIF). Note: CMYK images are automatically converted to RGB (PNG, JPEG, WEBP, BMP, TIFF, GIF). Note: CMYK images are automatically converted to RGB
• Printing: print selected pages or all pages
• Extraction: extract specific pages
• Metadata tab: displays information from ComicInfo.xml file (if present in archive). If you add or remove a page from a comic containing metadata, the application will offer to update the page count indicated in it. Other metadata cannot be modified. This limitation is intentional and results from a design choice.
help.config_files_content:
MosaicView creates a file named .mosaicview_config.json. This file contains some parameters necessary for the reset button to function properly, as well as the list of recently opened comics which is obviously necessary for displaying recently opened files. MosaicView also saves temporary files, which are necessary for various image processing operations.
All these files are located in the {temp_dir} directory.
Temporary files are deleted each time the application is closed, but the .mosaicview_config.json file, by its nature, is meant to be kept.
However, you can manually delete these files by going to the {temp_dir} directory, or by clicking the buttons below. The .mosaicview_config.json file will be recreated each time the application is opened, and temporary files will be recreated as needed.
help.config_temp_cleared: Temporary files have been successfully deleted.
help.config_config_cleared:
Configuration file has been successfully deleted.
It will be recreated on next application startup.
help.language_content:
MosaicView is available in multiple languages.
By default, the application automatically detects your operating system's language and displays in that language if supported. If your system uses an unsupported language, the interface displays in English.
You can manually change the language at any time using the dropdown menu located at the bottom of the button column, below the thumbnail size slider. Your choice will be saved and applied the next time you start the application.
AVAILABLE LANGUAGES: Arabic, Armenian, Bulgarian, Chinese (Simplified), Chinese (Traditional), Croatian, Czech, Danish, Dutch, English, Estonian, Finnish, French, German, Greek, Hindi, Hungarian, Icelandic, Indonesian, Irish, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Latvian, Lithuanian, Malay, Maltese, Norwegian, Polish, Portuguese, Romanian, Slovak, Slovenian, Spanish, Swedish, Tamil, Thai, Turkish, Ukrainian, Vietnamese.
FICTIONAL LANGUAGES AVAILABLE: klingon (Latin alphabet and pIqaD alphabet versions), sindarin (Latin alphabet and tengwar alphabet versions), quenya (Latin alphabet and tengwar alphabet versions).

This utility uses Windows fonts, except for pIqaD (Klingon language) and tengwar (Elvish languages: Sindarin and Quenya) fonts. These fonts are free of rights and come from these sites:
- for pIqaD (Klingon language alphabet): {url_piqad}
- for tengwar (Elvish alphabet, for Sindarin and Quenya): {url_tengwar}
In case these sites are no longer active, you can retrieve the pIqaD and tengwar fonts by clicking on the buttons below:

This software was developed in French. Translations into other languages were done using AI, specifically Claude. I decided to support around forty languages. But obviously, I don't speak all these languages. Therefore, I had no choice but to use AI, as hiring and paying 40 different translators was not among the possibilities considered. Due to the automatic nature of these translations, errors may be present. I apologize for this.
help.credits_content: I know nothing about programming. Absolutely nothing. Consequently, I developed this software thanks to Claude, Anthropic's AI, and more precisely with Claude Code. I cannot recommend Claude Code enough, this application is fabulous!
help.resize_pages_content:
To reduce or enlarge your pages:
• Select one or more pages
• Right-click → 'Resize'
• Three options are available:
  - Custom dimensions: enter the desired width or height (aspect ratio is automatically preserved).
  - Percentage reduction: choose a percentage from 10% to 90%
  - Percentage enlargement: choose a percentage from 10% to 100%
• The application displays the estimated size of your images after modification
• The modification applies immediately to all selected pages
help.icons_content:
Most of the icons used in this utility come from this site and are free to use for non-commercial purposes: https://www.freepik.com/author/juicy-fish/icons/juicy-fish-sketchy_908#from_element=resource_detail
Some have been modified by me.
Only one icon, the Windows executable icon, was created by me (but with AI, because I'm a very, very, very bad graphic designer). You have my permission to use this icon as you wish as long as you cite your source.
help.split_ui_content:
MosaicView allows displaying two archives simultaneously, side by side.

Click the Split icon to open a second panel. Each panel is independent: you can open, edit and save an archive separately. The active panel is indicated by a blue border.

To move pages from one panel to the other, drag them directly. If multiple pages are selected, they all move together. Pages are inserted at the position indicated by the red bar.

Click the Close second panel icon to return to single-panel mode.
help.config_clipboard_note: Exception: Temporary files used by the Windows clipboard (clipboard_XXXXX files) are kept for 12 hours after copying, so you can paste them even after closing the application. After 12 hours, they are automatically deleted.
help.config_log_note: Batch conversions (PDF to CBZ and CBR to CBZ) create a log file when errors occur. These files (Log_pdftocbz_.txt and Log_cbrtocbz_.txt) are kept even after closing the application, so they can be reviewed later. They are only deleted by the "Delete temporary files" button above.
help.config_clipboard_cleared: Clipboard files have been successfully cleared.
help.adjust_images_content: This function allows you to retouch your images directly in MosaicView. You can adjust brightness, contrast, sharpness, levels, saturation, apply effects (black and white, sepia, color inversion), adjust compression quality, remove colors, apply transparency or convert to different color depths. Some settings have a dedicated viewer allowing you to see the result in full resolution before applying. A thumbnail preview shows the result in real time in the main window.
help.create_icon_content:
MosaicView allows you to create icon files (.ico) from any image in the mosaic.
• Right-click on an image → 'Create an icon'
• Frame the desired area in the viewer (the icon will be square)
• Adjust transparency if needed
• Confirm: the .ico file is added directly to the mosaic
• .ico files automatically contain all standard resolutions (from 16×16 to 256×256)
• As long as a .ico file is present in the mosaic, saving to a CBZ archive is blocked, as the ICO format is not supported by these archives. Export the .ico file separately (Ctrl+C then paste into a Windows folder), then delete it from the mosaic before saving.
help.batch_convert_content:
MosaicView offers three batch conversion functions, available only when the mosaic is empty. For each of them, the principle is the same : select a directory, confirm, follow the progress in real time, review the final summary. An option allows permanently deleting the original files instead of sending them to the recycle bin.
• CBR → CBZ : converts all CBR (RAR) files in a directory and its subdirectories. If a CBR is actually a ZIP, it is simply renamed without conversion.
• PDF → CBZ : converts all PDF files in a directory and its subdirectories. Images are extracted without recompression.
• STANDALONE IMAGES → CBZ : creates an individual CBZ for each image file found in the directory and its subdirectories. Invalid, corrupted, multi-page (TIFF) or animated (GIF) images are ignored and logged.
help.icon_toolbar_content: The icon toolbar provides quick access to the main functions of the application. You can rearrange the icons as you wish by dragging them directly in the toolbar. To choose which icons to show or hide, click the gear button at the bottom of the toolbar: a configuration window opens where you can move icons between the active toolbar and the reserve. Use the « + » and « − » buttons to adjust the size of the icons. The configuration is saved automatically.

web.web_no_images_found: No images were found on this page.
web.web_copy_paste_message:
Direct drag & drop from web browser is not supported.

Alternative solution (simpler):

1. Right-click on the image in your browser
2. Choose 'Copy image'
3. In MosaicView, use the 'Paste' button or Ctrl+V

This method works with all web images!
web.import_web_tooltip: Paste an internet address to retrieve all images from the page
web.import_web_dialog_title: Import images from a web page
web.import_web_invalid_url: Invalid URL. Please enter a valid web address.

donation.title: Make a donation
donation.message:
You like this little utility, and you use it regularly. I've invested several hundred hours into it. Would you consider thanking me by making a donation? Be nice, I really need money to fill my swimming pool with champagne.

Whether you want to send me a few cents or several billion euros, I accept all amounts. You can make this donation through paypal

mail.tooltip: This button opens your default email software in Windows to send an email. If no software is configured (for example if you use only webmail), you can copy the MosaicView address to the clipboard by right-clicking on the icon.
